title: Bump Prometheus to v3.12.0 (currently pinned at v3.11.3)
summary: The newest rockcraft.yaml (3.11.3/rockcraft.yaml) pins Prometheus to v3.11.3, but upstream stable latest is v3.12.0. Bump version and source-tag, and create the matching version subdirectory.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained, mechanical version bump following the repo's existing per-version-subdirectory pattern; no architectural changes; clear upstream comparison.
affected_files: 3.11.3/rockcraft.yaml (version, source-tag); new 3.12.0/ subdirectory with rockcraft.yaml + spread tests
repro_result: rockcraft.yaml has version: "3.11.3" and source-tag: "v3.11.3"; upstream releases/latest = v3.12.0 (prerelease=false, published 2026-05-28). Rock lags upstream stable by one minor release.
fix_hint: Create a 3.12.0/ directory mirroring 3.11.3/ (copy rockcraft.yaml + spread/), set version: "3.12.0" and source-tag: "v3.12.0". Verify build-snaps go/node versions still apply for v3.12.0.
notes: Per-version subdirectory layout (2.x and 3.x dirs). Open issues #142/#139 are auto-generated CVE reports, not beginner-friendly. Issue #135 (run-user _daemon_) requires non-trivial debugging.

title: Add goss smoke test for promtool binary presence/version
summary: The root goss.yaml checks the prometheus process and three HTTP endpoints but does not validate the promtool binary, which the rock explicitly installs and organizes to /usr/bin/promtool.
why_good_first_issue: Small, well-scoped addition to an existing goss.yaml; easy to model on existing checks; teaches goss command/file checks.
repro_result: goss.yaml only has process:prometheus and three http: checks (config, buildinfo, targets). No command/file check for promtool, even though rockcraft organizes opt/prometheus/bin/promtool -> usr/bin/promtool.
fix_hint: Add a goss command check e.g. 'promtool --version' (exit-status 0) or a file check for /usr/bin/promtool existing and being executable.
notes: Optional second pick; lower priority than the version bump.
